data_description.camera_entity: Camera to capture snapshots from. After adding it, use 'Add trigger' on the integration page to create one or more timelapse triggers for it.

abort.already_configured: This camera is already set up. Add another trigger to the existing entry instead.

init.title: Camera configuration
init.description: Edit the quality defaults, or view and replace this camera's whole configuration as YAML.

quality.title: Video and image quality
quality.description: Defaults for every trigger on this camera. Individual triggers can override any of these.

data_description.video_quality: Encoding quality of the finished video. Higher quality means larger files and a slower render. Custom opens a step to set the raw x264 CRF and preset.
data_description.scale_mode: Off: frames and video keep the camera's native resolution. During capture: Home Assistant scales every snapshot the moment it is taken — this decodes and re-encodes each JPEG, costing CPU on every single frame for the whole session, but stores smaller frames on disk. During render: frames are stored at full size and ffmpeg downscales once while rendering the video (recommended unless frame disk usage matters).
data_description.max_width: Frames or video wider than this are scaled down, keeping the aspect ratio. Required when image scaling is enabled.

data_description.video_crf: x264 constant rate factor: lower is higher quality and a bigger file. 0 is lossless, 23 is the default, 51 is worst.
data_description.video_preset: Encoding speed versus compression efficiency. Slower presets produce smaller files at the same quality but render longer.

export_yaml.title: Export camera as YAML
export_yaml.description:
The whole camera configuration — its quality defaults and every trigger — as YAML. Copy it to back up, share, or edit and paste back via Import.

{yaml}

import_yaml.title: Import camera from YAML
import_yaml.description:
Paste a whole-camera YAML document (an 'options' mapping and a 'triggers' list, as produced by Export). This fully replaces the camera's triggers: triggers are matched by name, and any existing trigger not in the document is deleted. You confirm the deletions on the next step.

Renaming a trigger deletes the old name and recreates it under the new one. Trigger names must be unique within the document.

import_confirm.title: Confirm import

error.max_width_required: Enter a maximum width to enable image scaling.
error.invalid_condition: A conditional rule has invalid conditions: {error}
error.duplicate_names: Trigger names must be unique. Duplicated: {error}

user.title: Add trigger
user.description: Build the trigger step by step, or paste a YAML definition.

data_description.name: Used for the device name and as the name placeholder in filenames.
data_description.trigger_mode: Manual: start and stop with the switch or services. Daily time window: capture between two times every day. Entity state watch: capture while another entity is in chosen states.
data_description.capture_mode: Time interval: a snapshot every N seconds. Fit target video length: the interval is computed from a duration entity so the video always comes out about a chosen length. Entity value change: a snapshot each time a numeric entity moves by a chosen step, for example one frame per 3D-printer layer. Conditional: rules with Home Assistant conditions decide which cadence applies, with a default for when no rule matches.
data_description.output_dir: Folder for finished videos, created automatically if it does not exist (for example /media/timelapses). Leave empty for the default media folder. Anything under /media shows up in the Media Browser, including NAS shares added via Settings > System > Storage. Paths outside a media folder must be listed in allowlist_external_dirs.
data_description.filename_pattern: Pattern for the video filename. Wrap the placeholders name, timestamp or entry_id in curly braces to insert them, as in the default value.
data_description.keep_frames: Keep the individual snapshot frames on disk after the video is rendered.
data_description.auto_purge: Automatically delete old frame sets after each render and once a day. Only applies when Keep frames is on. Videos are never deleted.
data_description.purge_mode: Keep the N most recent sessions' frames, or delete frames older than N days.
data_description.purge_keep_sessions: Number of most-recent sessions whose frames are kept. Older sessions' frames are deleted.
data_description.purge_max_age_days: Frames in sessions older than this many days are deleted.
data_description.video_quality: Use service default keeps the camera entry's setting. Higher quality means larger files and a slower render. Custom opens a step to set the raw x264 CRF and preset.
data_description.scale_mode: Use service default keeps the camera entry's setting. Off: frames and video keep the camera's native resolution. During capture: Home Assistant scales every snapshot the moment it is taken — this decodes and re-encodes each JPEG, costing CPU on every single frame for the whole session, but stores smaller frames on disk. During render: frames are stored at full size and ffmpeg downscales once while rendering the video (recommended unless frame disk usage matters).
data_description.max_width: Frames or video wider than this are scaled down, keeping the aspect ratio. Only applies when this trigger also sets its own image scaling mode.

hub.title: Edit trigger
hub.description: Pick a section to change. Jump straight to what you need — you don't have to step through everything. Choose “Save changes” when you're done.

data_description.name: Used for the device name and as the name placeholder in filenames.
data_description.trigger_mode: Manual: start and stop with the switch or services. Daily time window: capture between two times every day. Entity state watch: capture while another entity is in chosen states. Changing this opens the matching setup step.
data_description.capture_mode: Time interval: a snapshot every N seconds. Fit target video length: the interval is computed from a duration entity so the video always comes out about a chosen length. Entity value change: a snapshot each time a numeric entity moves by a chosen step, for example one frame per 3D-printer layer. Conditional: rules with Home Assistant conditions decide which cadence applies. Changing this opens the matching cadence step.
data_description.output_dir: Folder for finished videos, created automatically if it does not exist (for example /media/timelapses). Leave empty for the default media folder. Anything under /media shows up in the Media Browser, including NAS shares added via Settings > System > Storage. Paths outside a media folder must be listed in allowlist_external_dirs.
data_description.filename_pattern: Pattern for the video filename. Wrap the placeholders name, timestamp or entry_id in curly braces to insert them, as in the default value.

data_description.keep_frames: Keep the individual snapshot frames on disk after the video is rendered.
data_description.auto_purge: Automatically delete old frame sets after each render and once a day. Only applies when Keep frames is on. Videos are never deleted.
data_description.purge_mode: Keep the N most recent sessions' frames, or delete frames older than N days.
data_description.purge_keep_sessions: Number of most-recent sessions whose frames are kept. Older sessions' frames are deleted.
data_description.purge_max_age_days: Frames in sessions older than this many days are deleted.
data_description.video_quality: Use service default keeps the camera entry's setting. Higher quality means larger files and a slower render. Custom opens a step to set the raw x264 CRF and preset.
data_description.scale_mode: Use service default keeps the camera entry's setting. Off: frames and video keep the camera's native resolution. During capture: Home Assistant scales every snapshot the moment it is taken — this decodes and re-encodes each JPEG, costing CPU on every single frame for the whole session, but stores smaller frames on disk. During render: frames are stored at full size and ffmpeg downscales once while rendering the video (recommended unless frame disk usage matters).
data_description.max_width: Frames or video wider than this are scaled down, keeping the aspect ratio. Only applies when this trigger also sets its own image scaling mode.

data_description.schedule_start: Capture starts at this time every day.
data_description.schedule_end: Capture stops and the video is rendered at this time. Overnight windows (for example 22:00 to 06:00) are supported.

data_description.watch_entity: The entity that drives the capture, for example a 3D printer status sensor or a motion sensor. You pick its active states in the next step.

data_description.watch_states: Capture runs while the entity is in any of these states. When it leaves them (including becoming unavailable), the capture stops and the video is rendered.

data.end_buffer_mode: Keep capturing after the trigger ends
data.end_buffer_retrigger: If the trigger fires again during the buffer

data_description.end_buffer_mode: Off: stop and render as soon as the trigger ends. Extra frames or extra seconds: keep capturing that much longer before rendering. Only applies when the time window ends or the watched entity leaves its active states; manual stops always take effect immediately.
data_description.end_buffer_amount: How many extra frames or seconds to capture, depending on the mode above.
data_description.end_buffer_interval: Seconds between snapshots during the buffer, overriding the session cadence. Required with the entity-value-change cadence, which becomes time-based during the buffer. Leave empty otherwise to keep the session cadence.
data_description.end_buffer_retrigger: Resume: cancel the buffer and keep capturing in the same session (also debounces a flapping trigger). Finish: complete the buffer, render the video, then start a fresh session.

data_description.duration_entity: Entity that reports the expected total duration of the session, for example a 3D printer's estimated print time sensor.
data_description.duration_type: How to read the duration entity: as a number of seconds, minutes, or hours, or as an end-time timestamp (duration = end time minus now, computed once at session start).
data_description.target_length: Desired length of the finished video. The capture interval is computed once when the session starts (duration divided by frame rate times this length, never below 1 second) and stays fixed for the whole session.
data_description.fallback_interval: Seconds between snapshots when the duration entity cannot be read as a positive number at session start.

data_description.value_entity: Numeric entity that paces the frames, for example a 3D printer's current layer sensor, an energy meter, or a distance sensor.
data_description.value_delta: A frame is captured each time the value moves by at least this amount since the last frame. Use 1 for one frame per layer.
data_description.value_direction: Which way the value must move to capture. With increase or decrease, movement the other way re-baselines silently (a layer counter resetting for a new print just starts counting again).

conditional_rules.title: Conditional cadence
conditional_rules.description:
Rules are checked top to bottom; the first whose conditions all hold paces the frames for the whole session. The default is used when no rule matches.

{rules_summary}

rule_conditions.title: Conditions and cadence
rule_conditions.description: Editing {rule_label}. Choose the conditions that select this rule and which cadence paces frames while they hold. The cadence's own settings come next.

data_description.conditions: Home Assistant conditions that must all hold for this rule to apply, for example a numeric state condition on a layer count sensor.
data_description.capture_mode: Time interval: a snapshot every N seconds. Fit target video length: auto-compute the interval from a duration entity. Entity value change: a snapshot each time a numeric entity moves by a chosen step.

data_description.interval: Seconds between snapshots while this rule applies.
data_description.duration_entity: Entity that reports the expected total duration of the session while this rule applies, for example a 3D printer's estimated print time sensor.
data_description.duration_type: How to read the duration entity: as a number of seconds, minutes, or hours, or as an end-time timestamp.
data_description.target_length: Desired length of the finished video. The capture interval is computed once when the session starts.
data_description.fallback_interval: Seconds between snapshots when the duration entity cannot be read as a positive number at session start.
data_description.value_entity: Numeric entity that paces the frames while this rule applies.
data_description.value_delta: A frame is captured each time the value moves by at least this amount since the last frame. Use 1 for one frame per layer.
data_description.value_direction: Which way the value must move to capture.

data_description.video_crf: x264 constant rate factor: lower is higher quality and a bigger file. 0 is lossless, 23 is the default, 51 is worst.
data_description.video_preset: Encoding speed versus compression efficiency. Slower presets produce smaller files at the same quality but render longer.

yaml_export.title: Trigger as YAML
yaml_export.description:
This trigger's configuration as YAML. Copy it to back up, share, or edit and paste back via 'Edit as YAML'.

{yaml}

yaml_edit.title: Edit as YAML
yaml_edit.description: Paste a YAML trigger definition (as produced by 'View as YAML'). It is validated and then saved, replacing this trigger's settings.

error.path_not_allowed: This path is not absolute or not allowed. Add it to allowlist_external_dirs or use a media folder.
error.schedule_start_equals_end: Start and end time must be different.
error.delta_positive: The step must be greater than zero.
error.length_positive: The target length must be greater than zero.
error.buffer_amount_required: Enter how many frames or seconds to buffer.
error.buffer_interval_required: A buffer capture interval is required when the session can be paced by entity value changes.
error.conditions_required: Add at least one condition, or finish with the default cadence instead.
error.value_entity_required: Pick the entity that paces the frames for the value change cadence.
error.max_width_required: Enter a maximum width to enable image scaling.
error.invalid_condition: A conditional rule has invalid conditions: {error}

options.capture: During capture (CPU cost on every snapshot)
During capture (CPU cost on every snapshot)

options.keep_recent: Keep the N most recent sessions
options.max_age: Delete frames older than N days

start.name: Start capture
start.description: Starts a timelapse capture session for a trigger.

stop.name: Stop capture
stop.description: Stops the capture session and renders the timelapse video.

render.name: Render again
render.description: Renders the most recent retained frame set again (kept frames or a failed render).

cancel.name: Cancel capture
cancel.description: Aborts the capture session and discards its frames without rendering.

purge_frames.name: Purge frames
purge_frames.description: Deletes all retained frame sets for a trigger. Videos are kept.

device_id.name: Trigger
device_id.description: The timelapse trigger whose frames should be purged.

device_not_found.message: No Auto Time Lapse trigger found for device {device_id}.

entry_not_loaded.message: Auto Time Lapse entry {title} is not loaded.

no_frames.message: There are no retained frames to render. Capture a session first, or enable 'keep frames'.
